 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oqw4","title":"E2E Tests: Keyboard Shortcuts","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-avo. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for keyboard shortcuts functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Global Shortcuts
1. Cmd/Ctrl+K opens command palette
2. Escape closes modals/overlays
3. / focuses search
4. ? shows shortcuts help
5. G then H goes home

### Navigation Shortcuts
6. G then L goes to library
7. G then S goes to search
8. G then P goes to profile
9. G then B goes to billing

### Content Shortcuts
10. N creates new prompt
11. E edits current prompt
12. C copies prompt content
13. S saves to library
14. D deletes (with confirm)

### Selection Shortcuts
15. J/K moves up/down in lists
16. X toggles selection
17. Shift+click multi-select
18. Cmd/Ctrl+A select all

### Shortcut Help
19. Help modal shows all shortcuts
20. Shortcuts grouped logically
21. Searchable shortcuts list

## Files to Create
- e2e/shortcuts/global.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/navigation.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/content.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/shortcuts-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- All shortcuts work reliably
- No conflicts with browser shortcuts
- Help accessible everywhere

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oyg6","title":"Implement public status page and incident management","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cr0q.

## Task
Create a public status page showing service health and incidents.

## Why This Matters
- Transparency with users
- Reduce support tickets
- Professional SaaS expectation
- Incident communication

## Features

### 1. Status Page
- Overall system status
- Individual component status
- Uptime percentage (30/90 days)
- Response time metrics

### 2. Components to Monitor
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Web App | Main application |
| API | REST API endpoints |
| Authentication | Login/signup |
| Database | Data operations |
| Search | Search functionality |
| Payments | Stripe integration |
| Email | Transactional emails |

### 3. Status Levels
- Operational (green)
- Degraded Performance (yellow)
- Partial Outage (orange)
- Major Outage (red)
- Maintenance (blue)

### 4. Incident Management
- Create incident
- Update incident status
- Post-incident report
- Incident history
- Subscribe to updates

### 5. Scheduled Maintenance
- Announce maintenance windows
- Countdown to maintenance
- Automatic status change
- Completion notification

## Implementation

### Status Check
```typescript
interface ComponentStatus {
  name: string;
  status: 'operational' | 'degraded' | 'partial' | 'major' | 'maintenance';
  latency?: number;
  lastChecked: Date;
}

async function checkComponentHealth(component: string): Promise<ComponentStatus> {
  switch (component) {
    case 'database':
      return await checkDatabaseHealth();
    case 'api':
      return await checkAPIHealth();
    // ... other components
  }
}
```

### Database Schema
```typescript
export const incidents = pgTable('incidents', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  title: text('title').notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(), // investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved
  impact: text('impact').notNull(), // none, minor, major, critical
  affectedComponents: text('affected_components').array(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'),
});

export const incidentUpdates = pgTable('incident_updates', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  incidentId: uuid('incident_id').references(() => incidents.id).notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(),
  message: text('message').notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const statusSubscribers = pgTable('status_subscribers', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  email: text('email').notNull().unique(),
  confirmedAt: timestamp('confirmed_at'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## Public Pages
- /status - Main status page
- /status/history - Incident history
- /status/subscribe - Email subscription

## Admin Pages
- /admin/status - Manage status
- /admin/incidents - Manage incidents
- /admin/maintenance - Schedule maintenance

## Integration
- Integrate with uptime monitoring (nelm)
- Automatic incident creation on outage
- Webhook to Slack/Discord

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/status/page.tsx
- src/app/api/status/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/status/page.tsx
- src/lib/status/health-checks.ts
- src/lib/status/incidents.ts

## Success Criteria
- Status page always accessible
- Real-time status updates
- Incident communication clear
- Subscription system works

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-p9c1","title":"Implement user suspension and ban workflow","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0798.

## Task
Implement structured workflow for user suspensions and bans.

## Why This Matters
- Handle violations consistently
- Progressive discipline
- Document actions
- Enable reinstatement

## Discipline Levels

### 1. Warning
- First offense for minor issues
- No access restriction
- Logged in history
- Educational message

### 2. Temporary Suspension (1-30 days)
- Moderate violations
- Cannot access account
- Content hidden
- Auto-reinstates

### 3. Indefinite Suspension
- Serious violations
- Manual review required
- Appeal possible
- Content preserved

### 4. Permanent Ban
- Severe/repeat violations
- Account deleted
- Content removed
- IP tracked (optional)

## Implementation

### Moderation Actions Table
```typescript
export const moderationActions = pgTable('moderation_actions', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // Target
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid('content_id'), // If content-specific
  contentType: text('content_type'),
  
  // Action
  actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // warning, suspension, ban
  severity: integer('severity').notNull(), // 1-4
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  internalNotes: text('internal_notes'),
  
  // Duration (for suspensions)
  startsAt: timestamp('starts_at').defaultNow(),
  endsAt: timestamp('ends_at'),
  
  // Tracking
  performedBy: uuid('performed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  
  // Reversal
  reversedAt: timestamp('reversed_at'),
  reversedBy: uuid('reversed_by'),
  reversalReason: text('reversal_reason'),
});
```

### User Status Check
```typescript
async function checkUserStatus(userId: string): Promise<UserStatus> {
  const activeActions = await db.select()
    .from(moderationActions)
    .where(and(
      eq(moderationActions.userId, userId),
      isNull(moderationActions.reversedAt),
      or(
        isNull(moderationActions.endsAt),
        gt(moderationActions.endsAt, new Date())
      )
    ))
    .orderBy(desc(moderationActions.severity));
  
  if (activeActions.length === 0) return { status: 'active' };
  
  const mostSevere = activeActions[0];
  return {
    status: mostSevere.actionType,
    reason: mostSevere.reason,
    endsAt: mostSevere.endsAt,
  };
}
```

### Enforcement
- Check status on login
- Block suspended users
- Show suspension message
- Redirect to appeal page

## Admin UI
- User lookup with history
- Take action button
- History timeline
- Reinstate option

## Notifications
- Email when action taken
- Appeal instructions included
- Reinstatement notification

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/moderation.ts
- src/lib/moderation/enforcement.ts
- src/app/(auth)/suspended/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/users/[id]/moderate/page.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Actions applied correctly
- Enforcement prevents access
- History tracked
- Reinstatement works

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rhvk","title":"E2E Tests: Network Resilience and Offline Mode","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2b44. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for network failure handling and offline mode.

## Why This Matters
- Users have bad connections
- Mobile users go offline
- Must handle gracefully
- No data loss

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Network Failure Handling
```typescript
test('should show error message on network failure', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Simulate offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Try to load more content
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Load More' }).click();
  
  // Should show offline message
  await expect(page.getByText(/network.*error|offline/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should retry failed requests', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Intercept and fail first request
  let requestCount = 0;
  await page.route('**/api/prompts**', async (route) => {
    requestCount++;
    if (requestCount === 1) {
      await route.abort('connectionfailed');
    } else {
      await route.continue();
    }
  });
  
  // Should auto-retry and succeed
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-list')).toBeVisible();
  expect(requestCount).toBe(2);
});
```

### 2. Offline Indicator
```typescript
test('should show offline indicator when disconnected', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should show offline indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).toBeVisible();
  
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should hide indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).not.toBeVisible();
});
```

### 3. Offline Data Access (PWA)
```typescript
test('should serve cached prompts when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  // First, visit while online to cache
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id');
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should still show cached content
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  await expect(page.getByText(/offline.*cached/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should queue actions when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id/edit');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Make an edit
  await page.getByLabel('Title').fill('Updated Title');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save' }).click();
  
  // Should show "queued" message
  await expect(page.getByText(/saved.*sync/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go back online
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should sync automatically
  await expect(page.getByText(/synced/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
});
```

### 4. Slow Network Handling
```typescript
test('should show loading states on slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate slow network
  const client = await page.context().newCDPSession(page);
  await client.send('Network.emulateNetworkConditions', {
    offline: false,
    downloadThroughput: 50 * 1024, // 50kb/s
    uploadThroughput: 50 * 1024,
    latency: 2000, // 2s latency
  });
  
  await page.goto('/search');
  await page.getByPlaceholder('Search').fill('test');
  await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
  
  // Should show loading skeleton
  await expect(page.getByTestId('search-skeleton')).toBeVisible();
});

test('should timeout and show message on very slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate extremely slow network
  await page.route('**/api/**', async (route) => {
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 30000));
    await route.continue();
  });
  
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Should show timeout message
  await expect(page.getByText(/taking.*long|timeout/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 });
});
```

### 5. Reconnection Handling
```typescript
test('should automatically reconnect and refresh', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should show reconnection message
  await expect(page.getByText(/back online|reconnected/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Should refresh data
  await expect(page.getByTestId('last-updated')).toContainText(/just now/i);
});
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log network state changes
- Log retry attempts
- Log cache hits/misses
- Screenshot error states

## Files to Create
- e2e/network/failures.spec.ts
- e2e/network/offline.spec.ts
- e2e/network/slow.spec.ts
- e2e/network/reconnection.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Errors shown clearly
- Retries work
- Offline mode functions
- Reconnection smooth

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ri69","title":"Fix touch target sizes to meet 44px minimum","description":"# Task: Fix Touch Target Sizes

## Context
Apple and Google HIG recommend minimum 44x44px touch targets for comfortable interaction. Several components violate this guideline.

## Specific Violations

### 1. SpotlightSearch Category Pills (lines 517-543)
**Current:** `py-1.5` ≈ 28px height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ...">
```

**Fix:** Increase to `py-2.5` for ~40px, or add min-height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-2.5 min-h-[44px] rounded-full text-sm font-medium ...">
```

### 2. BasketSidebar Remove Button (lines 223-229)
**Current:** `h-7 w-7` = 28px
```tsx
<Button variant="ghost" size="icon" className="h-7 w-7 shrink-0">
  <X className="h-3 w-3" />
</Button>
```

**Fix:** Increase to `h-9 w-9` = 36px (or h-11 w-11 = 44px)
```tsx
<Button 
  variant="ghost" 
  size="icon" 
  className="h-9 w-9 shrink-0 -mr-2" // Negative margin to maintain visual spacing
  aria-label={`Remove ${prompt.title} from basket`}
>
  <X className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

### 3. SpotlightSearch Close Button (mobile, lines 497-504)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-8 rounded-lg ...">
```

**Fix:** Already close, bump to `size-10` or `size-11`
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-11 rounded-lg ...">
```

### 4. SpotlightSearch Semantic Toggle (lines 480-494)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
**Fix:** Bump to `size-10` for touch friendliness

## Pattern for Touch-Friendly Buttons
```tsx
// Utility class for consistent touch targets
// Could add to globals.css:
.touch-target {
  @apply min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px];
}

// Or use Tailwind directly:
className="min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] ..."
```

## Design Considerations
- Larger touch targets may require layout adjustments
- Use negative margins to maintain visual density if needed
- Icon size should scale proportionally (but not required)
- Consider adding `touch-manipulation` class for faster response

## Visual Impact
- SpotlightSearch category pills will be slightly taller
- Basket remove buttons will have more breathing room
- Overall feel: more native/mobile-friendly

## Testing
1. Test on actual mobile device (simulator isn't accurate for touch)
2. Verify tap accuracy on category pills
3. Check remove button in basket with full list
4. Ensure no layout overflow issues
5. Compare before/after screenshots

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx

## Dependencies
- Independent of color changes
- May affect overall mobile layout slightly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:03.618658128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:03.618658128-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rij8","title":"E2E Tests: Cookie Consent & Analytics Gating","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-1gte. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for cookie consent UX and analytics script gating.

## Test Scenarios

### First Visit
- Banner shows on first visit (required regions)
- Accept All enables analytics scripts
- Reject All prevents analytics scripts
- Customize allows per-category toggles

### Persistence
- Consent stored and persists across reloads
- Version change forces re-consent

### Privacy Signals
- GPC/DNT defaults to analytics off
- Settings link accessible from footer

## Files to Create
- e2e/privacy/consent-banner.spec.ts
- e2e/privacy/consent-settings.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/consent-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Consent UX works end-to-end
- Analytics gated correctly
- Privacy signals respected

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rnm","title":"Implement useLocalStorage hook","description":"**Details:**
- Generic hook for localStorage persistence
- SSR-safe (handles hydration)
- Type-safe with generics
- Used by: basket, theme, variable inputs, preferences

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts
'use client';

export function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T) {
  // State to store our value
  const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState<T>(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return initialValue;
    try {
      const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;
    } catch {
      return initialValue;
    }
  });

  // Persist to localStorage on change
  useEffect(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(storedValue));
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn('Failed to save to localStorage:', error);
    }
  }, [key, storedValue]);

  return [storedValue, setStoredValue] as const;
}
```

**SSR Handling:**
- Initial render uses provided default
- useEffect syncs with localStorage after hydration
- Prevents hydration mismatch errors

**Location:** apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts

**Reasoning:** Many features need persistent state (basket, theme, variable inputs). Centralizing localStorage logic in a hook ensures consistent behavior and SSR safety.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:35.714509321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","close_reason":"Added apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts SSR-safe localStorage hook"}
